executive_summary: Iteration 000027 achieves near-complete compliance with the lofi design system PRD. All three user stories are substantially implemented: the lofi color palette and typography tokens are consistently applied across the UI via CSS custom properties and Tailwind utilities, the parameter controls panel has clear visual hierarchy with appropriate spacing and type styles, and the visual scene panel is properly framed with lofi borders and shadow rings. A single minor deviation was found: the queue empty-state paragraph ('No generations yet.') uses Tailwind's raw stone-300 color instead of the lofi-accentMuted token, representing a one-line oversight that is the only remaining non-lofi color reference in the main application. All functional requirements (FR-1 through FR-5) are satisfied: CSS custom properties remain the single source of truth, the Tailwind config exposes all lofi utilities, changes are purely visual, functional behavior is intact, and the config and index.css are in sync.

verification_by_fr.notes: All six lofi CSS custom properties (--color-lofi-bg, --color-lofi-panel, --color-lofi-accent, --color-lofi-accent-muted, --color-lofi-text, --color-lofi-shadow-ring) are defined exclusively in src/index.css. No duplicate color literals for these values were found in component files. Shadow values are consumed via var(--color-lofi-shadow-ring) in inline styles where Tailwind cannot express inset shadows.

tailwind.config.ts correctly extends the colors object with a lofi key mapping bg, panel, accent, accentMuted, and text to their CSS custom property references. All lofi token classes (bg-lofi-*, text-lofi-*, border-lofi-*) are consumed as utilities across components with 92 total usages.

All changes in this iteration are purely visual (CSS/Tailwind class updates). No logic, state, or API modifications were introduced. Disabled and loading states were already implemented; only their visual styling was updated.
Audio generation, image upload, playback, queue, and live mirror functionality remain unaffected. The implementation only touches className strings and CSS token values.

tailwind.config.ts lofi color extension references var(--color-lofi-*) which matches the variable names defined in src/index.css exactly. Font families (Nunito as sans, Merriweather as serif) are also consistently defined in both files.

verification_by_us.notes: AC01–AC09 pass: bg-lofi-bg/bg-lofi-panel are used universally; borders use lofi tokens; interactive elements have consistent accent hover/focus states; tab active indicator uses border-lofi-accent; app title uses font-serif text-4xl font-bold; subtitles use text-sm text-lofi-accentMuted; body text uses text-sm with Nunito; text-xs is absent from primary content (only used on the live-page metadata badge). AC10 partially fails: one raw stone-300 reference remains at App.tsx line ~992 in the queue empty state ('No generations yet.'), which should be text-lofi-accentMuted.

AC01–AC07, AC09–AC11 pass: section headings use consistent text-sm font-semibold text-lofi-text; heading hierarchy is uniform; labels are visually distinct from inputs; control groups have gap/padding ≥ 8px; Generate button is full-width with clear disabled/loading states; error messages use red-tinted styling and loading uses accent-tinted border; queue entries have well-spaced badge, brief, and action; queue list is scrollable. AC08 partially fails: the empty queue message uses text-stone-300 instead of text-lofi-accentMuted.

AC01–AC06 all pass: scene panel uses bg-lofi-panel with border-lofi-accentMuted and an inset shadow via var(--color-lofi-shadow-ring); aspect-ratio container centers the canvas correctly at all viewport sizes; play/pause buttons use border-lofi-accent and bg-lofi-accent/20–25; seek slider track uses the gradient from index.css with lofi token colors and no raw gray overrides; lint/typecheck pass; no element bleeds outside the panel frame.

minor_observations: App.tsx ~line 992: empty queue state uses `className="text-sm text-stone-300"` — should be `text-lofi-accentMuted` to satisfy AC08 (US-002) and AC10 (US-001).

live-page.tsx uses `bg-black` and `text-white/70` for the fullscreen live preview, which is intentional and contextually appropriate (fullscreen broadcast context), but worth noting as a deliberate exception to the lofi palette.

visual-scene.tsx SVG fallback uses hardcoded hex values (#3a2b24, #12100f, #c08457, #8b5e3c) that match the lofi tokens numerically. These cannot reference CSS variables inside SVG fill attributes, so this is acceptable, but consider switching to a data-URI approach or a React-rendered SVG using currentColor if the palette ever changes.

Semantic color overrides (text-red-100 / border-red-400 for errors; text-emerald-100 / border-emerald-300 for success) are outside the lofi token set but serve a distinct communicative role. They are consistently applied and do not conflict with the palette.

All panel shadows are expressed via inline style boxShadow referencing var(--color-lofi-shadow-ring). This is necessary because Tailwind's ring/shadow utilities don't support arbitrary inset + outer compound shadows at this complexity level. The approach is correct but could be extracted to a Tailwind plugin or CSS component class in a future iteration to reduce inline styles.
conclusions_and_recommendations: The iteration is largely successful. The lofi design system is coherently applied across the entire UI with one trivial exception. The single actionable fix is: change `text-stone-300` to `text-lofi-accentMuted` in the empty queue state message in App.tsx (≈ line 992). This change alone will bring all user stories and acceptance criteria to full compliance. No structural or architectural changes are required. As a lower-priority follow-up, consider: (1) adding the SVG fallback palette to the lofi token definition so it stays in sync if colors change; (2) creating a Tailwind CSS component class for the compound panel shadow to eliminate the repeated inline-style pattern across scene and controls panels.
